about.title: About Velohood
about.body1: My name is Nazar, and I am the developer behind Velohood. My main idea was to give everyone the ability to list their bicycle for rent — and to help small bike rental companies offer easy online booking to their customers.
about.body2: Velohood is a peer-to-peer marketplace where owners earn from bikes they are not using every day, and renters skip the rental shop markup and deal directly with local people. Owners set their own prices and availability; renters book directly with them.
about.body3: For me, this project is one small way to make the world a little better — built by people, for people.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body1: This project is currently maintained by a single developer as a student project.
contact.ticketBody: Logged in? Open a tracked ticket instead of email — you'll get a reply right here and can follow the status.
contact.body2: For questions, bug reports, or feedback, feel free to reach out by email.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Velohood is a student project. The information below explains how your data is handled on this platform. This is not a substitute for formal legal advice.
privacy.section1Body: We collect the information you provide when creating an account (first name, last name, email address, and optionally a phone number and profile photo), as well as bike listings you create, booking requests, messages sent through the in-app chat, and condition photos uploaded during a rental.
privacy.section2Body: Your data is used solely to operate the marketplace: to process bookings, display bike listings, enable communication between renters and owners, send email verification links and notifications, and manage your account.
privacy.section3Body: We do not sell your data or share it with third parties for advertising. Information visible to other users (such as your name, profile photo, and reviews) is limited to what is needed to complete a rental. Bike listing details are publicly visible to allow renters to find and book bikes.
privacy.section4Body: All accounts require email verification before activation. Our team monitors bike listings and can remove any that violate platform rules, and accounts that violate those rules can be suspended or permanently banned by an administrator. Passwords are stored in encrypted form and never in plain text.
privacy.section5Body: You can update or delete your profile information at any time from your account settings. To request full account deletion or ask a question about your data, contact us by email via the Contact page.
privacy.section6Body: For questions about privacy or data, reach out via the Contact page.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms apply to your use of Velohood. This is a student project and these terms may not cover every legal scenario, but they outline the responsibilities of both bike owners and renters on the platform.
terms.section1Body: Velohood is a marketplace that connects bike owners and renters. Velohood itself is not the owner of any listed bike and is not a party to the rental agreement between users. Disputes between users are resolved directly between the parties involved.
terms.section2Body: By listing a bike, you confirm that: the bike is in safe, working condition; brakes, gears, handlebars, and tires function correctly and are safe to use; your listing photos accurately reflect the real condition of the bike; you have the right to rent out the bike; and the stated price and availability are accurate.
terms.section3Body: By renting a bike, you agree to: return it in the same condition in which it was received; handle the bike with reasonable care during the rental period. Normal wear (minor surface scratches from ordinary use) is not the renter's responsibility. Damage beyond normal wear — including damage from an accident, broken parts, or deliberately caused damage — must be compensated by the renter to the owner. The bike must be returned by the agreed time, or any extension coordinated with the owner in advance.
terms.section4Body: A booking request is not confirmed until the owner explicitly accepts it. Both parties can communicate through the in-app chat to coordinate pickup, return, and any issues. Either party may cancel before the rental period begins.
terms.section5Body: Users must not post false or misleading listings, misuse the chat, or engage in harassment or fraud. Accounts found to be in violation may be suspended or permanently banned. Users can report violations using the report feature on any listing or user profile.
terms.section6Body: These terms may be updated as the project evolves. Continued use of the platform after any update constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

howItWorks.title: How Velohood works
howItWorks.subtitle: A peer-to-peer marketplace connecting bike owners with people who want to rent — here's the full flow for both sides.
howItWorks.forRentersSubtitle: Three steps from search to ride.
howItWorks.renterStep1Desc: Search by city and dates to see what's available near you, with real photos and prices set by the owner.
howItWorks.renterStep2Desc: Pick your dates, add an optional message, and send a booking request to the owner.
howItWorks.renterStep3Desc: Once the owner accepts, coordinate pickup in the in-app chat, then collect the bike and ride.
howItWorks.forOwnersSubtitle: Turn a bike you're not using every day into income.
howItWorks.ownerStep1Desc: Add photos, a description, and your price per day. Your listing goes live immediately — our team can still step in and remove it later if it breaks the rules.
howItWorks.ownerStep2Desc: Renters send booking requests for the dates they want — you choose which to accept.
howItWorks.ownerStep3Desc: After the rental, you and the renter review each other, building trust for future bookings.
howItWorks.safetySubtitle: Every booking is backed by features designed to protect both sides.
howItWorks.safetyPhotosDesc: Owners and renters can document the bike's condition at handover and return.
howItWorks.safetyReviewsDesc: Renters and owners rate each other after every completed booking — visible on profiles.
howItWorks.safetyChatDesc: Coordinate pickup details and ask questions through the in-app chat, no phone number needed.

trust.title: Trust & Safety
trust.intro: Velohood is built around a set of features designed to make rentals safe for both bike owners and renters. Here is how we protect both sides.
trust.section1Body: Every account must confirm their email address before it is activated. Anonymous or unverified accounts cannot place or accept bookings on the platform.
trust.section2Body: New bike listings go live immediately, but our team monitors listings and reactively removes or sends back for correction any that do not meet our standards — inaccurate photos, missing information, or bikes that are not in rentable condition.
trust.section3Body: After every completed rental, both the renter and the owner are invited to leave a review and rating. These are publicly visible on profiles and help users make informed decisions about who to rent to or from.
trust.section4Body: Any listing or user can be reported using the report button available throughout the platform. Reports are reviewed by an administrator, who can warn, suspend, or permanently ban accounts found to be in violation.
trust.section5Body: Owners and renters can upload before and after photos for each booking, documenting the bike's condition at handover and return. This creates a clear record in case of any dispute about damage.
trust.section6Body: If you have a concern not resolved through the platform's built-in tools, reach out to us through the Contact page.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions from renters and bike owners.
faq.bookingQ: How does booking a bike work?
faq.bookingA: Search for a bike by city and dates, then send a booking request to the owner with your chosen dates. The booking is only confirmed once the owner accepts it — you'll see the status update and can chat with the owner in the meantime.
faq.paymentQ: How and when do I pay?
faq.paymentA: Velohood is a peer-to-peer marketplace as a student project — payment is currently arranged directly between renter and owner, outside the app, once a booking is accepted.
faq.damageQ: What happens if the bike is damaged during the rental?
faq.damageA: Owners and renters can upload before/after condition photos for each booking to document the bike's state at handover and return, which helps resolve any disagreement about damage.
faq.cancelA: Yes — cancellable bookings show a Cancel button on your bookings page. Once cancelled, the dates become available again for other renters.
faq.verificationA: It means the account holder confirmed their email address via the link sent at registration. We're working on adding further verification (like phone and identity) in future updates.
faq.accessoriesQ: Can I rent accessories like a helmet or child seat?
faq.accessoriesA: Business owners can offer accessories — helmets, child seats, and locks — alongside their bikes. Availability depends on what the specific owner has listed.
faq.businessQ: I run a bike rental business — what's different for me?
faq.businessA: Upgrading to a Business account gives you a dedicated dashboard with bulk bike listing, a rental calendar across all your bikes, accessory management, and booking statistics.
faq.supportQ: I have another question — who do I contact?
faq.supportA: Reach out through the Contact page — this project is currently maintained by a single developer and questions are answered by email.

features.title: Why Velohood?
features.peerDesc: Rent directly from local owners. No middleman, fair prices.
features.flexibleDesc: Book for a day, a week, or longer. Cancel anytime before acceptance.
features.trustedDesc: Renters and owners rate each other after every completed booking.
